message:
feat(layout,render): add sequence participant group clusters and refine cluster title rendering

fm-layout: Implement sequence diagram participant group clustering —
groups of participants get bounding-box cluster boxes with titles.
Add explicit type annotation for cluster_bounds fold accumulator.
Reformat participant index collection for line length. Add test imports
for IrParticipantGroup and IrSequenceMeta. Add participant group cluster
test asserting title extraction.

fm-render-canvas: Add cluster title text rendering with proper
positioning and styling.

fm-render-svg: Refine cluster title rendering adjustments.

fm-render-term: Refine terminal cluster title rendering for
consistent placement.

Update beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.994240852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.643658982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: classDef is supported in mermaid-js for flowchart, class, state, and potentially other diagram types. The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.569465581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

-{"id":"bd-ckwu","title":"Sequence follow-up: layout/render participant group boxes from sequence_meta","description":"# Goal
Sequence `box ... end` groups are parsed into `IrSequenceMeta.participant_groups` but never laid out or rendered in the current pipeline, so the closed parity bead is not actually complete.

# Scope
- Convert `sequence_meta.participant_groups` into deterministic layout geometry in `fm-layout`.
- Render those group boxes across SVG, terminal, and canvas.
- Preserve deterministic ordering and nested-group behavior.
- Add focused tests proving `box` syntax produces visible output, not just parser metadata.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence `box` groups appear in layout/render output across all three renderers.
- Geometry is deterministic and encloses the intended participant headers/lifelines.
- Focused parser/layout/render regression tests cover the feature.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:38:52.401186772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T17:39:00.965653515Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","render","sequence"]}

 {"id":"bd-ct6f","title":"Sankey: Implement flow color gradients and value-proportional rendering","description":"# Sankey Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Flow bands should have color gradients (source color → target color)
- Flow width should be proportional to value (currently approximated)
- Node labels should be positioned outside the flow for readability
- Interactive hover highlighting (WASM only)

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement gradient fills on flow bands using linearGradient defs
2. Ensure flow band width exactly proportional to value/total_flow
3. Position node labels to the left of first-column nodes and right of last-column nodes
4. Add data attributes for WASM hover interaction","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:10.808759311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31.619408492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","render","sankey"],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-ct6f","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

1. Two-node sankey with value 100 → verify flow band width proportional to value

 {"id":"bd-dcjc","title":"Polyhedral optimization for layout algorithm loop nests","description":"Apply polyhedral optimization (graveyard section 6.5) to crossing minimization and coordinate assignment inner loops. Cache-optimal traversal patterns.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:04.637547948Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","frankenmermaid","layout"]}
 {"id":"bd-drav","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram interaction fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break)","description":"# Sequence Interaction Fragments

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>Bob: Request
  loop Every minute
    Bob->>Alice: Heartbeat
  end
  alt Success
    Bob->>Alice: 200 OK
  else Failure
    Bob->>Alice: 500 Error
  end
  opt Optional
    Alice->>Carol: Forward
  end
  par Parallel
    Alice->>Bob: Task 1
  and
    Alice->>Carol: Task 2
  end
  critical Critical Section
    Alice->>Bob: Important
  option Timeout
    Alice->>Bob: Retry
  end
  break When error
    Alice->>Bob: Abort
  end
  rect rgb(200, 220, 240)
    Alice->>Bob: Highlighted
  end
\`\`\`

Fragment types: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, critical/option, break, rect (colored background).
Each fragment is a labeled box spanning the involved participants, enclosing the messages within.
Fragments can be nested.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Fragment keywords produce warnings.
- No layout support for fragment regions.
- No SVG rendering of fragment boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::FragmentStart { kind, label }, FragmentElse { label }, FragmentEnd. Track nesting depth. Kind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect.
2. **IR**: Store as Vec<IrSequenceFragment> with kind, label, start_message_index, end_message_index, participant_range, and nested children.
3. **Layout**: Fragments add padding around their contained messages. Compute fragment box bounds from the first/last message y-positions and the leftmost/rightmost participant x-positions. Nested fragments get progressively larger bounds.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw fragment boxes as dashed-border rectangles with a label tab in the top-left corner. Fragment kind shown in bold in the tab. 'else'/'and'/'option' dividers shown as dashed horizontal lines within the fragment.
5. **Tests**: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, nested fragments, rect with color.

## Complexity
This is the single most complex sequence diagram feature. Fragment nesting requires a stack-based parser and recursive layout. Budget 2-3x the effort of a simple task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.719718813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.360084926Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.359593546Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence interaction fragment parsing (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect). Stack-based parser tracks nesting depth. Fragments store kind, label, start/end edge indices, alternatives (for else/and/option dividers), and nested child indices. 'end' keyword dispatches to close either fragments or box groups. 6 tests added covering all fragment types, alt/else alternatives, nested fragments, rect with color, and warning-free parsing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.838352205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-drav","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:29.797041627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-drav","author":"ubuntu","text":"SCOPE NOTE: This bead is intentionally large because the fragment parser, layout integration, and renderer are tightly coupled. Splitting parser from renderer would create a half-baked intermediate state (parsed but invisible fragments). Implementors should budget 2-3x normal effort. Suggested approach: implement loop first (simplest fragment), then alt/else (adds alternation), then the rest. Each fragment type can be tested incrementally.

DETAILED TEST CASES:
1. Simple loop: loop Every minute / Bob->>Alice: ping / end → verify dashed box with 'loop' tab, contains 1 message
2. Alt with else: alt OK / A->>B: 200 / else Error / A->>B: 500 / end → verify two sections separated by dashed line
3. Nested: loop / alt / A->>B: x / else / A->>B: y / end / end → verify nested boxes with correct containment
4. Par with and: par / A->>B: t1 / and / A->>C: t2 / end → verify parallel sections
5. Rect with color: rect rgb(200,200,240) / A->>B: x / end → verify colored background
6. Empty fragment: loop Empty / end → verify empty box renders without crash
7. Fragment spanning all participants: verify box width covers leftmost to rightmost participant involved
8. 3-level nesting: loop / critical / opt / A->>B / end / end / end → verify 3 nested boxes","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
